markets.question: New Rihanna Album before GTA VI?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Rihanna officially releases a new album before Grand Theft Auto VI is officially released in the US. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If neither occurs by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to 50-50.

For the purposes of this market, "release" refers to the game becoming publicly available for purchase or download in the US. Early access, beta versions, other forms of pre-release availability, or leaks will not count as an official release. If the release is only for certain consoles (e.g. Xbox Series X/S) it will count.

The resolution source for the release of GTA VI is official information from Rockstar Games or its parent company, Take-Two Interactive.

Officially released means that a newly released album is officially available for download or streaming (not including live events) by the resolution date.

Re-releases, deluxe editions, compilations, or greatest hits albums will not count, unless they contain at least 50% tracks that have never been officially released in any format prior to the album's release.

The resolution source for the album release will be any official streaming or download site, e.g. Apple Music or Spotify.

events.title: What will happen before GTA VI?
events.description: This is a market on predicting events that will occur before the release of Grand Theft Auto VI.

eventMetadata.context_description: Rockstar Games confirmed GTA VI's release for November 19, 2026, following an earlier delay from a May target, establishing a clear window for pre-launch events that traders monitor closely. This extended timeline heightens focus on marketing milestones such as potential summer 2026 trailer drops, Take-Two earnings updates reaffirming the schedule, and any last-minute polish announcements. Market-implied odds reflect trader consensus on high-profile releases like new albums or AI model launches fitting within the period, influenced by industry patterns where Rockstar's campaigns build steadily toward launch. Historical delays underscore uncertainty around exact timing, while upcoming financial reports and promotional activity could shift sentiment on which cultural or tech moments precede the game.

markets.question: Will Harvey Weinstein be sentenced to no prison time?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the prison sentence, if any, imposed on Harvey Weinstein as part of his ongoing New York retrial (People of the State of New York v. Harvey Weinstein) by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

This market will resolve immediately based on the first sentence rendered in this case, regardless of any appeals. If Weinstein is found not guilty, there is a mistrial, or if the first sentencing does not include any jail or prison time, this market will resolve to "No Prison Time."

If no sentencing takes place by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will also resolve to "No Prison Time."

If the sentence falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

For the purposes of this market, it does not matter whether the sentence is concurrent or consecutive to any existing sentence Weinstein is already serving. The market will resolve based on the total prison sentence imposed in this case.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the New York court system or other involved U.S. government sources; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent mistrials in Harvey Weinstein's New York rape retrial, including the latest deadlock on May 15, 2026, combined with ongoing appeals of his 2025 New York sexual assault conviction and 2022 Los Angeles rape verdict, have fueled trader consensus around No Prison Time as the frontrunner. The 73-year-old former producer's documented health decline and six-plus years already served at Rikers have shifted focus toward potential plea resolutions, concurrent sentencing, or successful appeals that could limit or eliminate further incarceration. Prosecutors' hesitation on a fourth trial and skeptical reception to his California appeal arguments underscore the contested nature of additional time, with upcoming sentencing hearings and appeal rulings as key near-term catalysts for any momentum shifts.

markets.question: Will the Carolina Hurricanes win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Carolina Hurricanes win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve to “No” if it becomes impossible for this team to win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup based off the rules of the NHL.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the NHL.

events.title: 2026 NHL Stanley Cup Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NHL Stanley Cup championship.

eventMetadata.context_description: Carolina Hurricanes hold a 58.5% implied probability as 2026 Stanley Cup favorites over the Vegas Golden Knights at 41.4%, driven by their dominant Eastern Conference playoff run and superior regular-season standing. Carolina posted a 53-22-7 record for 113 points, secured the top seed in the East, and advanced to the Final with a 12-1 postseason mark after quick series wins over Ottawa, Philadelphia, and Montreal. Vegas, despite a lower 39-26-17 mark for 95 points, reached the Final via upsets including a series win over Presidents' Trophy-winning Colorado but faces home-ice disadvantage in Raleigh for Game 1 on June 2. Trader consensus reflects Carolina's stronger overall form, depth, and historical edge in similar matchups against this opponent.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Spain and France sit nearly tied atop the field, reflecting their status as European champions and deep rosters stocked with elite talent across multiple positions. Spain’s blend of experience from recent Nations League and Euro success plus emerging stars has kept its implied probability just ahead, while France’s consistent depth and defensive structure maintain parity despite minor squad adjustments. England’s strong qualifying campaign and attacking options place it a clear third, with Brazil, Argentina, and Portugal clustered just behind on historical pedigree and current form. The expanded 48-team format and completed qualifiers have introduced more variance, but the concentration of elite European and South American sides sustains a tightly contested race where small edges in preparation and matchups could shift outcomes rapidly.

markets.question: Xi Jinping out before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if China's General Secretary of the Communist Party, Xi Jinping, is removed from power for any length of time between July 3, 2025, and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

CCP General Secretary Xi Jinping will be considered removed from power if he announces his resignation from his role as General Secretary, or is otherwise dismissed, detained, disqualified, or otherwise loses his position or is prevented from fulfilling his duties as General Secretary within this market's timeframe.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

events.title: Xi Jinping out before 2027?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if China's General Secretary of the Communist Party, Xi Jinping, is removed from power for any length of time between July 3, 2025, and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

CCP General Secretary Xi Jinping will be considered removed from power if he announces his resignation from his role as General Secretary, or is otherwise dismissed, detained, disqualified, or otherwise loses his position or is prevented from fulfilling his duties as General Secretary within this market's timeframe.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Xi Jinping maintains firm control over the Chinese Communist Party through ongoing elite purges in the military and Politburo, including the January 2026 removal of senior generals and subsequent ousters of top officials, which have eliminated potential rivals and reinforced personal authority ahead of the 2027 Party Congress. He has actively shaped the 15th Five-Year Plan launch for 2026–2030, emphasizing stability, technological self-reliance, and Party self-governance in public addresses and diplomatic engagements through early 2026. With no verified reports of health issues or organized opposition, these developments align with trader expectations that Xi will remain in power, consistent with his prior term-limit removal and historical patterns of sustained leadership.

markets.question: Will Gavin Newsom win the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Democratic Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democratic Party sources.

Any replacement of the democratic nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

events.title: Democratic Presidential Nominee 2028
events.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Democratic Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democratic Party sources.

Any replacement of the democratic nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Gavin Newsom maintains the strongest position in the wide-open 2028 Democratic presidential nomination market due to his term-limited status as California governor, high national visibility from repeated public clashes with the Trump administration, and early donor outreach. The fragmented field reflects limited consolidation so far, with Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez drawing support from progressive voters, Kamala Harris benefiting from recent general-election experience and book-tour visibility, and governors such as Josh Shapiro and Pete Buttigieg appealing through moderate profiles in key states. Recent Emerson College polling shows a competitive hypothetical primary, underscoring name recognition as the dominant early factor. Trader consensus could shift with 2026 midterm results, early-state polling trends, fundraising totals, or party-leader endorsements ahead of the first primaries.
